title: PADCEV Plus Keytruda Shows Strong Phase 3 Bladder Cancer Results
text: Pfizer and Astellas announced positive Phase 3 EV-304 results for PADCEV in combination with Keytruda in cisplatin-eligible muscle-invasive bladder cancer. The regimen reduced the risk of tumor recurrence, progression, or death by 47% compared with standard-of-care neoadjuvant gemcitabine and cisplatin. Estimated two-year event-free survival was 79.4% for the PADCEV plus pembrolizumab group versus 66.2% for standard chemotherapy. Overall survival also improved, with a 35% lower risk of death, and pathological complete response was 55.8% versus 32.5% for chemotherapy. Pfizer said the data, together with EV-303 results, support the potential for PADCEV plus pembrolizumab to become a new standard of care for muscle-invasive bladder cancer if approved. This news is useful RAG evidence because PADCEV came from Pfizer's Seagen acquisition and is an important oncology growth asset. Key risks include safety warnings, regulatory approval timing, competition, reimbursement, and whether clinical benefits translate into commercial growth.
